summary: The smallest viable DB-010 slice was checker enforcement for sqlite query boundaries plus richer Bun contract metadata derived from existing schema projection helpers; the compiler already carried enough projection/classification data to avoid broader refactors.
ergonomics: The emitted schema objects already had `projection()` and field helper metadata, which made it straightforward to derive row/field proof information in `runtime/bun/server.mjs` without touching codegen.
Checker rules for storage/sqlite surfaces are localized enough that extending mutation-style enforcement to queries was a one-function change.
The test suite is broad and reliable, but targeting a single Tasty case through `cabal test --test-options` is awkward because shell quoting/pattern parsing is easy to get wrong.
follow_ups: Unify the storage/sqlite proof metadata shape into an explicit shared contract helper so future runtimes do not reimplement it ad hoc.
Add direct assertions for protected `storage:*` bindings too; this task covered sqlite query/mutation end to end, but storage bindings now benefit from the same enriched contract shape indirectly.
Consider documenting the Bun contract proof fields if external tooling is expected to consume them.
warnings: Do not assume sqlite queries were already boundary-checked because mutations were; before this change `sqlite:queryOne`/`sqlite:queryAll` still accepted bare primitives.

When writing sqlite runtime tests that pass record params, SQL placeholders must match every named field the runtime sends or Node sqlite throws `Unknown named parameter` errors that look unrelated to the contract change.
`bash scripts/verify-all.sh` is noisy from repo-dirty warnings and benchmark artifact generation; absence of clean logs does not mean the verification failed, so rely on the final exit status.
residual_risks: The new proof metadata is emitted on Bun storage/sqlite contract descriptions only; if another runtime starts deriving its own storage contract shape, it will need to mirror the same row/field proof structure.
`bash scripts/verify-all.sh` still emits existing dirty-tree and Node `MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON` warnings during benchmark/example verification, but it completed successfully.
